name: Doubly Linked List Library
description: Implement a DoublyLinkedList class with advanced operations. The Node class stores value, next, and prev pointers. The DoublyLinkedList supports append, prepend, delete, reverse, merge sort, and merging two sorted lists. All pointer manipulations must maintain both next and prev references correctly.
tests:
def test_append_and_to_list():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.append(1)
    ll.append(2)
    ll.append(3)
    assert ll.to_list() == [1, 2, 3]

def test_prepend():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.prepend(3)
    ll.prepend(2)
    ll.prepend(1)
    assert ll.to_list() == [1, 2, 3]

def test_delete():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.append(1)
    ll.append(2)
    ll.append(3)
    assert ll.delete(2) == True
    assert ll.to_list() == [1, 3]
    assert ll.delete(5) == False

def test_reverse():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.append(1)
    ll.append(2)
    ll.append(3)
    ll.reverse()
    assert ll.to_list() == [3, 2, 1]

def test_sort():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.append(3)
    ll.append(1)
    ll.append(4)
    ll.append(1)
    ll.append(5)
    ll.sort()
    assert ll.to_list() == [1, 1, 3, 4, 5]

def test_len_and_contains():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.append(10)
    ll.append(20)
    assert len(ll) == 2
    assert 10 in ll
    assert 30 not in ll

def test_merge_sorted():
    l1 = DoublyLinkedList()
    l1.append(1)
    l1.append(3)
    l1.append(5)
    l2 = DoublyLinkedList()
    l2.append(2)
    l2.append(4)
    l2.append(6)
    merged = merge_sorted(l1, l2)
    assert merged.to_list() == [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

def test_empty_operations():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    assert ll.to_list() == []
    assert len(ll) == 0
    ll.reverse()
    assert ll.to_list() == []
    ll.sort()
    assert ll.to_list() == []

def test_doubly_linked():
    ll = DoublyLinkedList()
    ll.append(1)
    ll.append(2)
    ll.append(3)
    ll.reverse()
    ll.reverse()
    assert ll.to_list() == [1, 2, 3]
constraints: Must use doubly-linked nodes (prev and next pointers)
sort must use merge sort algorithm
merge_sorted must not modify input lists
All operations must work correctly with empty lists
